name: June Analytics API
what_it_does: Provides programmatic access to June's B2B product analytics platform, enabling agents to query company-level and user-level feature adoption, retention, and churn metrics from a Segment-compatible event stream.
use_cases: Query company-level feature adoption rates to identify accounts at risk of churn and feed them into a CRM workflow
Retrieve retention cohort data by company segment to generate automated weekly product health reports
Track feature adoption milestones for specific accounts to trigger onboarding nudges or success team alerts
Pull active user counts per company to populate a customer success dashboard with real-time engagement signals
List companies that have not used a key feature in 30 days to build a re-engagement outreach list
not_for: Consumer or B2C analytics at scale — June is designed for B2B SaaS with company-level grouping, not individual consumer products with millions of anonymous users
Raw event ingestion at high volume — June is built on top of Segment or direct SDK tracking; it is not a replacement for a full event collection infrastructure
Complex custom SQL queries against raw event data — June provides opinionated B2B metrics, not a flexible query engine like Amplitude or Mixpanel
best_when: Best when an agent is working with a B2B SaaS product and needs company-level behavioral analytics — feature adoption, retention, and churn signals grouped by account rather than individual user.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need deeply customizable funnel analysis, raw event exports, or are building a consumer product where individual user-level granularity at scale is required.

auth.notes: API key authentication via HTTP header. Keys are generated in the June workspace settings. No granular scope control — keys grant full read access to workspace data.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier available for early-stage teams with limited tracked companies and events per month.
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans scale with the number of tracked companies and monthly tracked users. Pricing not publicly listed — requires contacting June.
pricing.notes: June targets early-stage and growth-stage B2B SaaS teams. Pricing is usage-based and negotiated; the free tier is generous for small products.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits not prominently documented; standard API fair-use limits apply. Contact June for high-volume programmatic access requirements.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is relatively thin for programmatic use; error messages follow HTTP conventions but lack detailed remediation guidance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only reporting endpoints are fully idempotent. Safe to retry without side effects.
known_agent_gotchas: June's API surface is smaller than Amplitude or Mixpanel — not all metrics visible in the UI are queryable via API; agents should verify endpoint availability before building workflows
Company identification depends on group() calls in the event tracking implementation — if the underlying tracking is missing group associations, company-level queries return incomplete data
Data freshness can lag 1-4 hours behind real-time; agents polling for very recent activity may see incomplete results without realizing it
The API is oriented toward B2B company metrics and may not expose the raw event tables needed for custom funnel analysis — agents expecting Mixpanel-style flexibility will be disappointed
API documentation may not reflect the latest features; some newer endpoints are undocumented and only discoverable through the June team or changelog

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys lack granular permissions. TLS enforced. No OAuth flow for agent-based access — single workspace-scoped key model is simple but not least-privilege.
